# embed-code-ts тАФ Full Documentation for AI Crawlers

## Project Overview

embed-code-ts is a TypeScript monorepo providing ONNX Runtime-based code embeddings using the nomic-embed-code model (int8 quantized). It enables semantic code search, RAG, and code understanding directly in Node.js and browsers with zero network dependency post-install.

**Organization**: AgentiX-E
**Repository**: https://github.com/AgentiX-E/embed-code-ts
**Homepage**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/
**License**: Apache 2.0
**Node.js**: тЙе22
**TypeScript**: тЙе5.0

## Architecture

```
Input Text
  тЖТ WordPiece Tokenizer (40,856 vocab, max 512 tokens)
  тЖТ ONNX Runtime Inference (int8 quantized BERT-base, 137M params)
  тЖТ Mean Pooling (attention-mask-aware)
  тЖТ L2 Normalization
  тЖТ 768-dimensional Float32Array embedding
```

## Package Details

### @agentix-e/embed-code-core (v0.1.1)
- **Description**: Zero-dependency TypeScript core тАФ WordPiece tokenizer, pooling, normalization, platform-agnostic interfaces
- **Size**: ~50 KB (minified)
- **Dependencies**: None
- **Key exports**: `WordPieceTokenizer`, `loadVocab`, `preTokenize`, `meanPool`, `l2Normalize`, `cosineSimilarity`, `processBatch`, `IEmbedder`, `IOrtBackend`, `IOrtSession`, `IOrtTensor`, `EmbedCodeError` and error subclasses
- **API Docs**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/api/modules/embed-code-core_src.html

### @agentix-e/embed-code-node (v0.1.1)
- **Description**: Node.js ONNX Runtime inference engine with native AVX2/AVX-512 acceleration
- **Dependencies**: `@agentix-e/embed-code-core`, `onnxruntime-node` (тЙе1.20.0)
- **Key exports**: `NodeEmbedder` (implements `IEmbedder`), `NodeEmbedderOptions`
- **Features**: Zero-copy tensor operations, batch processing with concurrency control, progress callbacks
- **Hardware**: Requires x86_64 with AVX2 (AVX-512 for optimal performance)
- **API Docs**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/api/modules/embed-code-node_src_onnx-embedder.html

### @agentix-e/embed-code-web (v0.1.1)
- **Description**: Browser ONNX Runtime with WebGPU auto-upgrade and WASM fallback
- **Dependencies**: `@agentix-e/embed-code-core`, `onnxruntime-web` (тЙе1.20.0)
- **Key exports**: `WebEmbedder` (implements `IEmbedder`)
- **Features**: WebGPU acceleration when available, automatic WASM fallback, works in modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- **API Docs**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/api/modules/embed-code-web_src_onnx-embedder.html

### @agentix-e/embed-code-cli (v0.1.1)
- **Description**: Command-line tool for model download, embedding generation, and system info
- **Dependencies**: `@agentix-e/embed-code-core`, `commander` (тЙе15.0.0)
- **Commands**: 
  - `embed-code setup` тАФ Download and cache ONNX model (supports proxy with auth)
  - `embed-code embed "<text>"` тАФ Generate embedding for text or file (-f)
  - `embed-code info` тАФ Display model and system information
- **Proxy support**: `--proxy-url`, `--proxy-username`, `--proxy-password`, `EMBED_CODE_PROXY_PASSWORD`, `EMBED_CODE_PROXY_PASSWORD_FILE`
- **API Docs**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/api/modules/embed-code-cli_src_cli.html

## Model Details

- **Base architecture**: BERT-base (bert-base-uncased)
- **Layers**: 12 transformer encoder layers
- **Hidden dimension**: 768
- **Attention heads**: 12
- **Parameters**: 137,000,000
- **Vocabulary**: 40,856 WordPiece tokens with special tokens ([CLS], [SEP], [PAD], [UNK], [MASK])
- **Max sequence length**: 512 tokens (longer inputs truncated)
- **Quantization**: int8 dynamic quantization (weights only, activations in fp32)
- **Model size on disk**: ~137 MB (vs ~440 MB for fp32)
- **Precision impact**: Cosine similarity fp32 vs int8 > 0.995
- **Training dataset**: CoRNStack (arxiv.org/abs/2412.01007)
- **Source**: nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5 on HuggingFace

## Task Prefixes (Search Intent)

- **Query prefix**: `search_query: {text}` тАФ used when generating embeddings for search queries
- **Document prefix**: `search_document: {text}` тАФ used when indexing code/documents
- These prefixes were used during model training and are essential for optimal retrieval quality

## Performance Benchmarks

| Backend | Hardware | Latency (per token) | Throughput |
|---------|----------|---------------------|------------|
| onnxruntime-node | AVX-512 (e.g., AMD EPYC, Intel Xeon) | ~5ms | ~200 req/s |
| onnxruntime-node | AVX2 (e.g., Intel Core i7+, AMD Ryzen) | ~12ms | ~80 req/s |
| onnxruntime-web | WebGPU (Chrome/Edge with GPU) | ~20ms | ~50 req/s |
| onnxruntime-web | WASM (all browsers) | ~50ms | ~20 req/s |

Benchmark reports: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/benchmark/

## How to Install

```bash
# Core + Node.js
npm install @agentix-e/embed-code-core @agentix-e/embed-code-node

# Core + Browser
npm install @agentix-e/embed-code-core @agentix-e/embed-code-web

# CLI
npm install -g @agentix-e/embed-code-cli
```

## How to Use

### Node.js
```typescript
import { NodeEmbedder } from '@agentix-e/embed-code-node';
const embedder = await NodeEmbedder.create({ modelPath: './models/model.int8.onnx' });
const emb = await embedder.embed('search_query: binary search tree');
console.log(emb); // Float32Array(768)
await embedder.dispose();
```

### Browser
```typescript
import { WebEmbedder } from '@agentix-e/embed-code-web';
const tok = await fetch('/models/tokenizer.json').then(r => r.json());
const embedder = await WebEmbedder.create('/models/model.onnx', tok);
const emb = await embedder.embed('search_document: class Node { ... }');
await embedder.dispose();
```

### CLI
```bash
embed-code setup
embed-code embed "function sort(arr) { return arr.sort(); }"
embed-code embed -f src/index.ts
embed-code info
```

## CI/CD and Quality

- **Test coverage**: >95% line, branch, function, statement (no mocks, real model inference)
- **CI**: GitHub Actions (ci.yml, nightly.yml, release.yml, model-release.yml)
- **Linting**: ESLint with TypeScript strict, Prettier formatting
- **Pre-commit**: Husky + lint-staged
- **Coverage report**: https://agentix-e.github.io/embed-code-ts/coverage/

## Contributing

See CONTRIBUTING.md in the repository. Development requires Node.js тЙе22.

## FAQ

**Q: Can I use this without internet after install?**
A: Yes. The int8 model weights are bundled in the npm package. After `npm install`, everything works offline.

**Q: What code languages are supported?**
A: 20+ languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Rust, C/C++, Ruby, PHP, and more (trained on CoRNStack).

**Q: How accurate is int8 vs fp32?**
A: Cosine similarity exceeds 0.995 тАФ essentially lossless for retrieval.

**Q: Can I run this in serverless (Lambda, Cloud Functions)?**
A: Yes, pre-download the model at build time. Cold start <100ms with int8.

**Q: Does it support GPU inference?**
A: The browser package supports WebGPU. Node.js uses CPU (AVX2/AVX-512 optimized). For GPU in Node.js, use the original Python model.
